rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.,DBA GREAT VS. OAKLAND OPERA THEATER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 20, 2012, Line 1. PLAINTIFF CREDITORS TRADE ASSOCIATION, INC.'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Assignment Of Rights, Restraining Order And Turnover Order. Pro Tem Judge Brad Kaplan, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Counsel for Judgment Creditor should be prepared to address prior collection efforts and the specifics related to the need for the various orders requested in the motion. The Judgment Creditor should also bring a proposed order/ proposed orders to the hearing as none were provided with the courtesy copies of the brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REDWOOD MORTGAGE INVESTORS VIII, A CALIFORNIA VS. GEORGE HAUSER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 20, 2012, Line 8. CROSS COMPLAINANT 60 RAUSCH STREET HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. 60 Rausch HOA's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to all issues sought to be summarily adjudicated. The CC&Rs and the grant deeds are reasonably susceptible to the interpretations proffered by both sides and the extrinsic evidence and the inferences therefrom are in conflict and thus create a triable issue as to which side's interpretation of those documents correct. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

The moving party is to provide a proposed order as none was provided with the courtesy copy of the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to brad@kaplans.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL F. KLAPPER VS. GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED, AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 20, 2012, Line 21. DEFENDANT GENTRY PARTNERS LIMITED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first six causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action. Plaintiff has leave to amend to allege the existence of a tolling agreement or otherwise to allege why the first six claims are not time-barred and to clarify that the contract is oral. The seventh cause of action for declaratory relief is neither necessary nor proper and thus is sustained without leave to amend per Code of Civil Procedure section 1061. Defendant's remaining arguments lack merit. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: COMMUNITY TRUST CREDIT UNION VS. JUAN ESCOBAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 20, 2012, Line 26. Plaintiff's Order to Show Cause re Application for Pre-Judgment Writ of Possession. Although no opposition was filed, a hearing is required for the plaintiff to show, if it is able to do so, that it served defendants. If no one appears at the hearing or insufficient proof of service is shown, the temporary restraining order will be dissolved and the order to show cause taken off calendar.
